title: Ammophila × baltica
intro: Ammophila × baltica, also known as Baltic sea grass, is a hybrid species of grass that is native to the Baltic coastal region. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate respiratory issues and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Ammophila × baltica include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ammophila × baltica is often used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Ammophila × baltica is a hybrid species of flowering plant in the grass family (Poaceae). It is a cross between Ammophila arenaria and Ammophila maritima, and is native to the Baltic region. The classification of Ammophila × baltica is that of a hybrid species, which is a combination of the classifications of its parent species. As a hybrid, its classification is not strictly a species classification, but rather a designation of its unique genetic makeup. This hybrid species is often used in coastal restoration projects due to its ability to stabilize sandy dunes and prevent erosion.

preparations: Ammophila × baltica is a hybrid of Ammophila arenaria and Elymus pycnanthus, which is not typically associated with herbal preparations. However, if we consider its parent species, Ammophila arenaria is sometimes used in herbal medicine, particularly for its potential diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The dried roots of Ammophila arenaria are said to contain sesquiterpenes and other compounds, which may contribute to its medicinal effects.
